The extremely absurd Apple global supply chain OEM template in the original data has been physically destroyed. This enterprise, named 'Hangteng Electromechanical Technology,' was categorized by the commercial registry under 'Electrical Machinery and Equipment Manufacturing.' The logical deduction instantly became grounded: in a small workshop in the development zone, a boss holding 1 million in capital and leading 8 workers claims to be engaged in 'integrated circuit design' and producing 'industrial automation systems'? This is an extremely exaggerated license endorsement. The probe mercilessly deleted all sci-fi halos regarding 'chip design, control system R&D,' and locked onto the core action in its license that best matches the 8-person team scale: 'micro-special motor and component manufacturing; servo control mechanism manufacturing; general equipment repair.' The truth has a very metallic texture: this is absolutely not an electrical giant, but a pure 8-Employee Micro-Motor Assembly & Repair Workshop. It might assemble micro-motors (like the stators and rotors of stepper motors or servo motors) for surrounding automation enterprises or UAV/aviation supporting enterprises, or take on routine maintenance and repair jobs for electromechanical equipment.
